reasoning:

Saint Kitts and Nevis is a small island nation (population ~47,000) that is politically stable. The last notably violent political riots were in 1994, with some minor protests occurring over electoral issues in 2024. General elections took place in 2022 and are not required until 2027. There are ongoing concerns about youth gang violence, but gang violence is normally coded under "Battles" or "Violence against civilians", not "Riots" (which applies specifically to disorganized mobs engaging in violence).

Given the historical frequency of essentially 0 riots per year in the ACLED dataset for SKN, the base rate likelihood for any specific 30-day window is very low (less than 2%). The 30-day window for June 1, 2025, covers May 2 to June 1. As of the forecast date (May 25), 23 of these 30 days have already elapsed without any known reports of riots in SKN. 

I assign extremely low probabilities for all time horizons. They increase slightly over time because there is inherently more uncertainty as we look further into the future (e.g., unexpected sudden catalysts might emerge by late 2025).
